The highway connects Bulatan, Kuala Nerus in the north to Bukit Besar, Kuala Terengganu in the south. It was named after Yang di-Pertuan Muda (Crown Prince) of Terengganu at that time, [Tengku Mizan Zainal Abidin](/wiki/Sultan_Mizan_Zainal_Abidin "Sultan Mizan Zainal Abidin") from 1979 to 1998 (now [Sultan Mizan Zainal Abidin](/wiki/Mizan_Zainal_Abidin_of_Terengganu "Mizan Zainal Abidin of Terengganu")). It has been a toll-free highway since toll collection was abolished on 1999.[[1]](#cite_note-:0-1)[[2]](#cite_note-2)

**Jalan Tengku Mizan**, [Federal Route 65](/wiki/Malaysia_Federal_Route_65 "Malaysia Federal Route 65") (formerly Terengganu State Route T4), is a major highway in [Kuala Nerus](/wiki/Kuala_Nerus "Kuala Nerus") and [Kuala Terengganu](/wiki/Kuala_Terengganu "Kuala Terengganu"), [Terengganu](/wiki/Terengganu "Terengganu"), [Malaysia](/wiki/Malaysia "Malaysia"). The highway connects Bulatan, Kuala Nerus in the north to Bukit Besar, Kuala Terengganu in the south. It was named after Yang di-Pertuan Muda (Crown Prince) of Terengganu at that time, [Tengku Mizan Zainal Abidin](/wiki/Sultan_Mizan_Zainal_Abidin "Sultan Mizan Zainal Abidin") from 1979 to 1998 (now [Sultan Mizan Zainal Abidin](/wiki/Mizan_Zainal_Abidin_of_Terengganu "Mizan Zainal Abidin of Terengganu")). It has been a toll-free highway since toll collection was abolished on 1999.[[1]](#cite_note-:0-1)[[2]](#cite_note-2)

## Features

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Jalan_Tengku_Mizan&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Features")]

The highway has a [motorcycle lane](/wiki/Motorcycle_lane "Motorcycle lane"). Landmarks along the highway include [Sultan Mahmud Bridge](/wiki/Sultan_Mahmud_Bridge "Sultan Mahmud Bridge") and [Terengganu Welcome Sign](/wiki/Terengganu_Welcome_Sign "Terengganu Welcome Sign") *(Allah Peliharakanlah Terengganu)*.[[1]](#cite_note-:0-1) The highway also known as one of most busiest route during festival.[[3]](#cite_note-3)

At Sultan Mahmud Bridge, 4 artworks featured at the pillars.[[4]](#cite_note-4)

The highway also used for Marathon race.[[5]](#cite_note-5)
